evals.prompt: Use /okf init on this repo. It has docs/plans/payments-redesign.md, docs/architecture.md, adr/0001-use-postgres.md, README.md, and AGENTS.md. Reorganize the existing docs into an OKF bundle under ./docs, keep root files that tooling expects in place, and add instructions to AGENTS.md for future agents.
evals.expected_output: Creates an OKF-compliant ./docs bundle with specs and adrs sections, preserves root README/AGENTS files, adds frontmatter to concept docs, writes index/log files, creates Markdown cross-links between related concepts, and adds AGENTS.md instructions for reading and maintaining the OKF bundle.

evals.prompt: We are at the end of a feature branch that added SSO login, changed the auth boundary, and added a migration. Use /okf update so future agents understand what changed. The repo already has docs/index.md, docs/specs/index.md, and docs/adrs/index.md.
evals.expected_output: Reads the current OKF map, updates relevant specs and roadmap entries, adds or updates an ADR for the auth boundary decision, refreshes Markdown cross-links between related concepts, updates migration or runbook docs if useful, and records concise newest-first log entries.

evals.prompt: Initialize OKF docs for a small CLI repo that currently only has README.md, CHANGELOG.md, and a docs/usage.md file. Do not overbuild the taxonomy. I mainly want future coding agents to know where specs, ADRs, and durable guides live.
evals.expected_output: Creates the required OKF shape with docs/index.md, docs/log.md, docs/specs, and docs/adrs; treats docs/usage.md as a guide/reference concept with frontmatter; keeps optional sections minimal; updates AGENTS.md or proposes creating it when missing.

evals.prompt: Use /okf read before touching the billing code. I want to know which roadmap items, specs, ADRs, and runbooks are relevant to the invoice retry work.
evals.expected_output: Performs a read-only traversal starting from docs/index.md and docs/specs/index.md, follows relevant cross-links into specs, ADRs, runbooks, guides, reference docs, or domain concepts, then summarizes loaded context, constraints, docs gaps, and recommended next files without modifying logs or docs.
